Name: A$AP
Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server:
(2006) (A$AP Mob is founded by A$AP Yams, A$AP Bari and A$AP Illz)
(2007) (Rocky joins the A$AP Mob, officially taking on the name A$AP Rocky)
(January-April 2008) (A$AP Bari introduces A$AP Rocky to A$AP Yams at a party)
(~2008) (A$AP Rocky slowly drifts away from MDB, eventually leaving)
(September-December 2008) (six months later Rocky contacts Yams telling him he wants to get serious about music)
(May 10th, 2010) ("Been Around The World" is released, and featured on the front page of OnSmash.com, giving him a reputation in the Harlem area)
(October 23rd, 2010) (The final 'leak' is posted to the HarlemzMostHated YouTube channel, "Bittersweet Symphony Part 2")
Type: Sometime during 2006, while Rocky was still an active member in 'MDB', another Harlem based group was founded, entitled the 'A$AP Mob'. The collective was founded by A$AP Yams, A$AP Bari and A$AP Illz while together in SoHo as Bari explains, "I was like, 'Yo, we're always trying to come up with different names and shit. We should just be called PIF Unit.' The shit was like, Paid In Full, something. So one day, Yams was like, 'Fuck that. We're called A$AP'". The monicker 'A$AP' is an acronym meaning to 'Always $trive And Prosper', a saying Yams had gotten from another forgotten founder of A$AP unfinished WIP. Rocky joined the A$AP Mob in 2007. Rocky met A$AP Yams in 2008. In 2010, Yams would begin to release throwaways on the YouTube channel “HarlemzMostHated" while acting like he didn't know who Rocky was to try and gain fame, which would be featured on a fan-made mixtape titled “Deep Purple” which was released on April 22nd, 2011 and is commonly confused to be Rocky’s first project. While this compilation isn't official, it began to give Rocky more traction with his music.

Name:
💎 Been Around The World [V1]
(prod. Sunny J Beats)
Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server: Rocky's original semi-breakout track and first official song known to have been put out by Rocky, rather than A$AP Yams "unofficially" uploading the song onto YouTube, titled "Been Around The World". The song was released on May 10th, 2010 and was very notably featured on the front page of a popular music website at the time, OnSmash.com with an article by LEGEND. Due to its popularity in the early 2010s, this article and release gained Rocky his first bit of local popularity in the Harlem area, which A$AP Yams posted on Twitter about saying, "yo my artist Rocky ASAP just made the OnSmash front page". Shortly after this release of the song, A$AP Yams started to "unofficially" release other Rocky songs on his HarlemzMostHated YouTube channel, acting as if he did not know who Rocky was to try and gain hype for him and "Been Around The World" was possibly later released on the HarlemzMostHated YouTube channel around this time, but it is not confirmed as of now. The songs producer is currently completely unknown, but by the sound of the producer tag at the begining of the track it has been assumed to be "Sunny J Beats", or something very close to that. Sometime in 2010, producer DJ Burn One reposted "Been Around The World" on his blog at the time, which is likely how Rocky and Yams got the connection to him to make the songs "Houston Old Head" and "Roll One Up" in 2010. Later in the year the song was then sent to DJ DP by A$AP Yams on October 15th, 2010 to do an official "Slow Lane Remix" of the track, who DJ DP is known for making chopped and screwed tracks with this title. The remix was sent back just five days later and uploaded to the HarlemzMostHated channel "unofficially" on October 20th, 2010. On December 8th, 2010 A$AP Yams posted the file for the song on his then famous "realniggatumblr" Tumblr account with the name being "Been Around The World [Main]", a cover for the song with a screenshot of a naked woman the music video of Dr. Dre's song titled "Nuthin' But A "G" Thang" along with a description of the song which reads, "ASAP Rocky-“Been Around The World” (N/A, 2010) I remember snatching this up from DJ Burn One’s blog a while back and completely forgetting about this. Some smooth ass playa shit that’ll have the bitches giving up the grits. Just listen to that pimp ass guitar on the intro. The production on this is reminiscent of some older Timbaland stuff. I’m not usually a fan of these type of tracks untill they get the Chopped & Screwed treatment." Later on, the song was featured on the notorious fan-made mixtape "Deep Purple" created by a French fan of Rocky who goes by the name PuréBakingSoda, who owns a blog of the same name, was released on April 22nd, 2011 on his blog. The file of the song on the compilation was much worse quality than the original release for the song, making the high quality version lost for years. The song's original file high quality file that was sent to DJ DP to make the "Slow Lane Remix" was leaked by community member KILLRITE alongside the then completely lost and resurfaced "Been Around The World (Slow Lane Remix)" on January 2nd, 2024, and both of those files can be found in the 'Unreleased' tab of the Tracker. The song was later reused as track three of the May 16th, 2011 copy of Rocky's "Mouth Fulla Gold" mixtape along with the remix with no differences at all to the song released more than a year prior, along with the remix with no changes as well. The files that were used on that copy of the album were leaked by community members KILLRITE and Zanthin on July 18th, 2024 as a part of the successful $250 groupbuy for a copy of the "Mouth Fulla Gold" mixtape. On a copy of "Mouth Fulla Gold" from June 17th, 2011, the song and remix appeared on the tracklist with the original song as track two on the mixtape, in which the file leaked with the entire mixtape copy on February 28th, 2025. During the sessions for "LIVE.LOVE.A$AP", "Been Around The World" was being considered for the tracklist, as there are engineers that had sent the acapella and TV track to Rocky's label for a potential release, with this version of the song reportedly having a much higher quality instrumental compared to all other known versions of the song according to someone who owns this unreleased version.

Name:
⭐️ Uptown
(feat. Ty Nast (A$AP Nast) & Two Twelve Mel (A$AP Twelvyy)) (prod. ???) (co-prod. Arthur McArthur & Boi-1da)
Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server: A track titled "Uptown" which was the first song that was released onto A$AP Yams' HarlemzMostHated YouTube channel on May 28th, 2010, just a few weeks after A$AP Rocky's first song was featured on the front page of OnSMASH. The song is an ode to where Rocky is from, Harlem, New York (also known as 'Uptown'). The track has a hard-hitting instrumental by an unknown producer (producer tag: "Ayo, S you crazy for this one!) featuring a sped up sample of the song "Uptown" by Drake, Lil Wayne and Bun B (which samples "Uptown Girl" by Billy Joel), primarily using the beat and a small part of Lil Wayne's verse saying "I'm so uptown, and motherfucker if you ain't, don't go uptown, yeah". There are two guest features on the song including fellow A$AP Mob members A$AP Nast and A$AP Twelvyy who go both by the alternate names Ty Nast and Two Twelve Mel on this release for unknown reasons, although both were in the Mob at the time. The song was recorded earlier that year on January 22nd, 2010 based on a Tweet by A$AP Nast from that day stating "in da studio wit ASAP!!! UPTOWN our new track on da way!!!" signaling he was recording with the other two A$AP members. After the official release of the song it was featured on the fan-made "Deep Purple" compilation mixtape by PuréBakingSoda that was released on April 22nd, 2011. The track was reposted by both A$AP Nast and A$AP Twelvyy multiple times throughout 2011, and on September 23rd, 2021 Twelvyy brought the song up in his Discord server challenging members to answer "what is the first A$AP Mob song and what year was it released?" and stated "That’s the first mob song ever" after revealing the answer and posting a link for the track.

Name:
⭐️ Seth Narley - Grown Up [V1]
(feat. A$AP Rocky) (prod. The Olympicks)
Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server: The initial release of the song "Grown Up" by Bronx artist Seth Narley and A$AP Rocky was included as track fifteenth on Seth's "FreshMenNHighSkool" mixtape which was released on July 4th, 2010. The song samples the iconic Rick Ross "Mayback Music" tag on th intro which builds up into a luxurious instrumental produced by the Michigan based producer group The Olympicks (who notably also produced Rocky's song "Out Of This World"). This is the first known official collaboration between Rocky and Seth Narley who grew close around 2009 and 2010 as the two have even been confirmed to have lived together at some point, but it is unknown when this song was actually originally recorded. Months after the tracks release, a behind the scenes video of the song was posted on YouTube by sckrelloRS, a close friend of Seth's, on December 23rd, 2010 in a video titled "ASAP Rocky Ft Seth Narley  Grown Up BEHIND THE SCENES". In the description of this video it states "Off Of Seth Narley's FreshMenNHighSkool Mixtape an ASAP Rocky's Purple Swagg" insinuating that the song was not only intended for Seth's previously mentioned project, but was also to appear on a mixtape Rocky started working on just a few weeks earlier in December 2010 titled "Purple Swag" (aka. Purple Swagg" (which is believed to be the infamous 'deleted mixtape')) when he first collaborated with A$AP Ty Beats to make the song "Purple Swag". Much later on, the track was re-released as the outro, track eleven, on Seth Narley's "The Sigma Fly EP" on December 20th, 2011.

Name:
✨ Bittersweet Symphony
(host. Mr. LoOn3 & HitterCXC) (prod. Youth) (co-prod. The Verve)
Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server: The original version of the song "Bittersweet Symphony Part 2", more simpily titled "Bittersweet Symphony", was released on a mixtape by Mr. LoOn3 and HitterCXC titled "Interstate Mixtape Volume 1" which is a compilation of songs from artists from all around the United States with track six being "Bittersweet Symphony", with the song being listed as "Rocky ASAPNew York - Bittersweet Symphony". The tag stating "interstate mixtapes" during the the intro of this version of the song comes from this said mixtape, but it is unknown if a version without it exists. It is possible before the mixtape, which was released on a currently unknown release date, this said version without the tag was released. If the song was put out earlier it would have likely released on A$AP Yams' HarlemzMostHated YouTube channel at some point in 2010, but again it is unconfirmed if it does exist. The song itself is a has a direct sampleof the song "Bitter Sweet Symphony" by The Verve, which was produced by The Verve and Youth. In the song Rocky talks about the struggles in his life and overcoming them, and during the outro the track interpolates a Christian 18th century children's bedtime prayer known as "Now I Lay Me Down to Sleep" with some of the wording changed. After the song was released on "Interstate Mixtapes Volume 1" in 2010 a second part of the song was recorded where Rocky would re-record the his lyrics in a more upbeat tone with the same instrumental with the song being titled "Bittersweet Symphony Part 2". The second 'part' of the song was released on the HarlemzMostHated YouTube channel on October 23rd, 2010 with a few minor lyric changes and more upbeat tone. Later on, this first version of the song was featured on the notorious fan-made mixtape titled "Deep Purple" by a French fan named PureBakingSoda along with many other songs from this era instead of the second part on April 22nd, 2011. It is unknown why PureBakingSoda selected the original version of the song, but on the compilation the song was listed as "New York Bittersweet Symphony" likely due to a language barrier with the songs original listing being "Rocky ASAPNew York - Bittersweet Symphony", with New York just stating where Rocky is from rather than the title. Because of the mass popularity of this fan-made mixtape the name was thought to be real by many and stuck, but was never truly the title of the song.

Name:
💎 Get High
(feat. DEEFERG (A$AP Ferg)) (prod. Danger Mouse)
Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server: The song titled "Get High" was released on September 3rd, 2010 according to A$AP Ferg on his Tumblr stating "we just made a song called “GET HIGH” fuckin retarted". DEEFERG, as he went by then, released the full song on his Tumblr as well on the same day, September 3rd, 2010. The track then had then had a music video recorded and directed by King Zulu on A$AP Ferg's birthday on October 20th, 2010 and then released on November 27th, 2010. The tracks instrumental directly samples "Would Be Killer" by Gnarls Barkley and was possibly posted to the HarlemzMostHated YouTube channel at some point, but this is unknown. The song is featured on the notorious fan-made mixtape "Deep Purple" along with many other songs from Rocky's early career. In the now deleted April 21st, 2015 episode of Complex’s “Magnum Opus” series about the song "Purple Swag" that before the release "LIVE.LOVE.A$AP" Rocky wanted him and A$AP Ferg to be in a duo that went by the name of A$AP Red. It was then confirmed that him and Rocky worked on a mixtape under the title of "Rug Of War 1994" seemingly worked on under that alias, this song is the only confirmed track on the mixtape.

Name:
✨ Been Around The World (Slow Lane Remix)
(c&s. DJ DP) (prod. Sunny J Beats)
Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server: An official 'Slow Lane Remix' done by DJ DP to the song "Been Around The World" which was originally released on May 10th, 2010. The song was first sent to DJ DP by someone "on Rocky's team", who is rumored to be A$AP Yams, on October 15th, 2010 requesting for the song to be remixed along with stating the song would have a cover art with DJ DP's name on it, then the freshly-made remix was sent back to that person on October 20th, 2010 and was uploaded to A$AP Yams' HarlemzMostHated YouTube channel on the same day. The remix was completely lost until it resurfaced by KILLRITE on January 1st, 2024 with the OG file leaking on January 2nd, 2024 along with the OG file for the non-remix version of the song also by KILLRITE. The remix was reused as track eleven intended for the May 16th, 2011 copy of the May 16th, 2011 copy of Rocky's "Mouth Fulla Gold" mixtape along with the original song, these were the only 2010 songs on the mixtape. The files that were used on that copy of the album were leaked by KILLRITE and Zanthin on July 18th, 2024 as a part of the successful $250 groupbuy for the "Mouth Fulla Gold" mixtape.

Name:
💎 Bittersweet Symphony Part 2
(prod. Youth) (co-prod. The Verve)
Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server: Re-recorded version of the original "Bittersweet Symphony" which was posted on the HarlemzMostHated YouTube channel on October 23rd, 2010. Compared to the original, this song features a more upbeat flow and a few minor lyric changes, but features the same instrumental and sample of "Bitter Sweet Symphony". The outro is an interpolation of a Christian 18th century children's bedtime prayer known as "Now I Lay Me Down to Sleep" with some of the same wording changed from the previous version. After the song was released the original 'part one' of the song was used on the famous fan-made compilation "Deep Purple" by PureBakingSoda instead of this version for unknown reasons. Both versions of the song have been known as "New York Bittersweet Symphony" due to it being titled that on "Deep Purple". This is likely due to a language barrier as the creator is from France. The said miscommunication is from the original upload of "Bittersweet Symphony", the version that appeared on "Deep Purple", was listed as "Rocky ASAPNew York - Bittersweet Symphony", with the New York being an indicator of where Rocky is from, but the "New York Bittersweet Symphony" stuck due to the popularity of the compilation, but it was never officailly the title at any point.

Type: According to multiple A$AP Bari interviews, Rocky had a whole mixtape before "LIVE.LOVE.A$AP" but the engineer Rocky was working with deleted all of the songs and the only songs recovered were "Purple Swag", "Roll One Up" and "Demons". This means this project was deleted inbetween March 7th, 2011, the day the "Numb" instrumental was released by Clams Casino, which Rocky would later use to make "Demons" and April 14th, 2011, the day "Purple Swag" was released as a single and Rocky would not have to recover a released song. In another interview he mentioned "Get High" and "Toast To The Gods" were on the tracklist. It's unknown what the other songs that would have appeared are but those are lost forever. The cover included for this era is the single cover for the original version of "Houston Old Head".

Name:
✨ Houston Old Head [V1]
(prod. DJ Burn One)
Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server: Earliest version of the song "Houston Old Head" posted onto A$AP Yams’ infamous now deleted “realniggatumblr” account with the caption “This song is the soundtrack to those late nights after pouring up a couple deuces of that Ivan Ooze. There’s some irony about Rocky being from NY and making a track that in essence really favors Country Rap Tunes. It’s supposed to be a dedication track to an OG of his from Houston that was recently sentenced to life in prison. The highlight of the track is during the 2nd verse where he starts describing his OG reminiscing on his glory days, “And He Told Me Bout’ The Rap & Trappin In The Old Days/ When Pac Hung With Shock, Before The Rat-Tat-Tat & Nose Rings”. The song would appear later on Mouth Fulla Gold and would be reworked for the mixtape LIVE.LOVE.A$AP.

Name:
💎 Purple Swag
(prod. A$AP Ty Beats) (add. prod. A$AP Rocky)
Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server: The first public version of "Purple Swag" ever released "unofficially" on A$AP Yams' HarlemzMostHated YouTube channel on April 14th, 2011 with the title "ASAP Rocky-Purple Swag". In the description of the YouTube video it states "RIP BIG HAWK RIP FAT PAT RIP BIG MORE RIP DJ SCREW ALL MY LEAN SIPPERS WE HERE SHOUT OUTS TO MORGAN FREEMAN". During this time, A$AP Yams owned a famous, now deleted, Tumblr page entitled "realniggatumblr" where he shared "Purple Swag" hoping to get Rocky some popularity after his mixtape was deleted. On his Tumblr post Yams confirms the name of a mixtape Rocky was working on after his deleted and before "Mouth Fulla Gold". In the posts he stated "ASAP Rocky-"Purple Swag" (Me Myself And ASAP, 2011)" with a description which reads "It's NY Rap with a beat that doesn't sound tailor made for 106 & Park battles (hahaha thanks Cin for that description). No looped Jay-Z vocals on the chorus, no Ron Browz feature, no poor man's Swizz Beats production. Contemporary H-Town Rap music from an East Coast perspective, I think. From what I know the track is unfinished and will feature an H-Town rapper on it. That Michael Jones sample on the hook was the first time I heard from him since he was requesting for hoes to do push-ups for him with Hurricane Chris." The song would later be used on the "Mouth Fulla Gold" mixtape a month after.

Name:
💎 Toast To The Gods
(add. vox. A$AP Bari) (prod. Havoc) (add. prod. ???)
Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server: Song titled "Toast To The Gods" that was seemingly released online on April 28th, 2011. The song uses the instrumental to “The Future” by Diddy, produced by Havoc of Mobb Deep with additional instrumentation from an unknown producer. It has been said by Rocky himself that this is not only one of his favorite songs that he has done, but also a freestyle. Although he said this a long time ago he expressed that this song was right behind his verse on "Trilla". The song was confirmed to have been originally made for the mixtape predating Mouth Fulla Gold by A$AP Bari in a 2015 interview and was eventually seen again on several Mouth Fulla Gold tracklists. Something extra to note is that sometime in 2022 A$AP Nast promoted the song on his Instagram story.

Type: Following the deletion of his previous mixtape, Rocky began working on a new project titled "Mouth Fulla Gold" and had a copy of the mixtape ready on May 16th, 2011, which was sent out to select fans by A$AP Yams. During that month, likely around the time “Wassup” was released, Rocky stopped selling drugs and started take his brother's advice and truly started to focus on his rap career. On April 14th, 2011, Rocky released the song "Purple Swag" on the HarlemzMostHated YouTube channel. The track did not gain much recognition right away until it was put on the "Deep Purple" compilation as track three and had a music video release on July 5th, 2011, which led to the song becoming his first breakout hit. Rocky would continue to work on "Mouth Fulla Gold" as another copy was put together on June 17th, 2011. The same day the "Purple Swag" music video was released, A$AP Yams took to his now deleted Tumblr "realniggatumblr" and mentioned the name "LIVE.LOVE.A$AP" for the first time, which was then due at the end of July 2011.

Type: Following the hype and high expectations from the first single's music video, Rocky then released another single on July 21st, 2011 titled “Peso”, another hit under Rocky’s belt. From here, the mixtape was then delayed to August for unknown reasons. Rocky later 'leaked' an early version of "Trilla" on September 17th, 2011. In early October, Rocky signed a revolutionary two-year $3 million dollar record deal. Finally after all the waiting, on Halloween 2011 the project “LIVE.LOVE.A$AP” was officially released. This mixtape is widely known for its slowed down, Houstan influenced, psychedelic and dream-like sounds which made it stand out from many other hip-hop projects from this time period and it would become a gigantic pioneer for the genre of cloud rap for many years to come. Much later in 2021 the mixtape was re-released on streaming services, missing certain songs such as "Kissin' Pink" "Out Of This World" and "Purple Swag: Chapter 2" along with featuring different versions of the songs "Leaf" and "Acid Drip", but added a new song titled “Sandman” dedicated to the late A$AP Yams with production by long time collaborator Clams Casino along with a newer producer Kelvin Krash.

(prod. A$AP Ty Beats) (add. prod. A$AP Rocky)
Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server: The song "Purple Swag" was originally recorded in November or December 2010 when Rocky connected with A$AP Ty Beats for the first time, but was later released on A$AP Yams' HarlemzMostHated YouTube channel on April 14th, 2011 as a starting point for a new project, "Me, Myself & A$AP" after Rocky's mixtape at the time was suddenly deleted by an engineer, forcing Rocky to begin working on a new mixtape. For the first week of the songs release it did not get much attention until a fan named PureBakingSoda compiled many already released Rocky songs up to that point, including "Purple Swag" onto a fake mixtape titled "Deep Purple" and uploaded it to Datpff.com where the fake mixtape quickly started to gain attention. The song "Purple Swag" was notably the most popular song off the tracklist at the time which encouraged Rocky to make a music video for it. WIP desc

Name:
💎 Peso [V1]
(feat. Kevin Andreas) (prod. A$AP Ty Beats)
(Pe$o, Pesos)
Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server: In the music video for Rocky's song "Purple Swag" which was released on July 5th, 2011 a slowed snippet for an upcoming song was played. The song that was played was titled "Peso" and released as a single on July 21st, 2011. The song samples "No One's Gonna Love You" by The S.O.S. Band and was written by A$AP Rocky, Kevin Andres, Jimmy Jam, Terry Lewis and A$AP Ty Beats, mixed by Pat Viala and mastered by Dan Millice and Tatsuya Sato. The file that was uploaded to the A$AP Mob's LONGLIVEASAP Tumblr account revealed the song was originally intended for Rocky's "Mouth Fulla Gold" as a file with the metadata for that mixtape as track two of eleven. When the song was released it was intended for Rocky's then upcoming mixtape "LIVE.LOVE.A$AP". The track interpolates Rocky's own song "Wassup" as he reuses the "I be that pretty motherfucker, Harlem's what I'm reppin'" line. The song appeared as track two on "LIVE.LOVE.A$AP" which was released on October 31st, 2011. On December 8, 2015 singer Kevin Andreas stepped forward and stated that he wrote and sang the hook on the song. He stated that Rocky's voice was too deep to sing it so Kevin was asked to do it instead. Kevin never got paid for his controbutions as Rocky's busy schedule drove the two apart and he did not hear from him as Rocky went to a different studio to record. Since that time Kevin Andreas has tried to get paid for his contributions, but has seemingly still not done so.

✨ Purple Swag (Jim-E Stack Bootleg)
(rmx. Jim-E Stack) (prod. A$AP Ty Beats) (add. prod. A$AP Rocky)
Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server: A bootleg of A$AP Rocky’s song "Purple Swag" created by New Orleans producer Jim-E Stack, initially released September 16, 2011. The track transforms the original with hazy, slowed vocals layered over shuffled drums and smooth, flowing chords, giving it a club-ready, atmospheric sound. The song was then co-signed one day later by the Rocky and the A$AP Mob on September 17th, 2011 after the song was posted on the Mob's 'LIVELOVEA$AP' (later changed to 'LONGLIVEA$AP') Tumblr page with a caption reading "The homie Jim-E Stack from New Orleans went and provided us with some chillbeachwavepopsurfjams." with a now dead LimeLinx link. Later on, a music video for this bootleg was released on March 5, 2012 directed by Jason Ano, the director of the original "Purple Swag" music video. This video was labeled as Jason Ano's "Director's Cut" and features footage that was never included in the original music video. A few days later on March 9th, 2012 Rocky took to Twitter saying "VERY GROOVY, VERY TRIPPY, VERY JIGGY, VERY MELODIC PURPLE SWAG VIDEO BOOTLEG BY JIM-E STACK SHOUT OUTS TO JASON ANO", praising the video, Jim-E Stack and Jason Ano.

Name:

Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server: An alternate early version of the song "Trilla" was initially released on the A$AP Mob's 'LIVELOVEA$AP' (later changed to 'LONGLIVEA$AP') Tumblr page on September 18th, 2011 as a taste to A$AP Rocky's then upcoming "LIVE.LOVE.A$AP". The post on the 'LIVELOVEA$AP' Tumblr page was accompanied by a photo of the three featured artists on the song along with a title reading "A$AP ROCKY x A$AP TWELVY x A$AP NAST" and a caption that says "Since yall waiting on that LiveLoveA$AP we decided to go ahead and leak a new track to hold yall off till the the wave develops above sea level. Produced by the homie Beautiful Lou outta TX featuring the rest of the A$AP fam. Peep the live performance of Trilla HERE if you haven’t." which is referencing the September 8th, 2011 performance of the song at the Santos Party House where they previewed this same version. The song features laid-back and slow Texas style production from San Antonio native Beautiful Lou sampling “They Want EFX” by Das EFX and “Friendship Train” by The Undisputed Truth with two fellow A$AP Mob members alongside Rocky including A$AP Nast and A$AP Twelvyy who all lay down one verse each speaking about money, drugs, women, the A$AP Mob and much more. This version of the song features worse mixing and multiple parts during Rocky's verse that were rerecorded for the songs later release along with deep voice effects during Nast's verse that was also changed to have no effects on the later version of the song that was featured on Rocky's "LIVE.LOVE.A$AP" on October 31st, 2011.

Name:

(prod. A$AP Ty Beats)
Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server: Released online on September 19th, 2011 as a tribute to the famous Nike MAG shoes that were released on September 8th, 2011. These said shoes were made in reference to the 1989 film "Back to the Future Part II" as the shoes are a replica of the self-tying shoes in that movie. Seemingly, the track would have never seen a release if the Nike MAG shoes were not put out, but it is not confirmed if the song was intended for "LIVE.LOVE.A$AP" at the time of release. Metadata reveals that the song was recorded on December 7th, 2010, which means that it was likely made in the same 2010 sessions as the songs "Purple Swag", "Peso" and "Feeling Good" as they all share the same producer, A$AP Ty Beats.

Name:

(prod. Clams Casino)
(Base)
Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server: One of the songs played at Rocky's third live performance of his career at The Creators Project on October 15th, 2011 along with many other upcoming songs titled "Bass". The version of the song that was played live does not feature the intro and is seemingly better quality than the later version. That later version of the song in worse quaility and an added intro was released before the release of "LIVE.LOVE.A$AP" on the A$AP Mob's LONGLIVEASAP Tumblr account on October 28th, 2011. Just three days later the song was released as track three from Rocky's mixtape "LIVE.LOVE.A$AP" on October 31st, 2011 and track three on the re-release which was released on October 29th, 2021. The song was written by A$AP Rocky, Clams Casino and Imogen Heap, mixed by Pat Viala and mastered by Dan Millice and Tatsuya Sato. The tracks intro samples the YouTube video "Guy on Subway Flipping Out - the ORIGINAL Bloody Loco" by Nmboito along with "Shimmy Shimmy Ya" by Ol' Dirty Bastard. The last, and main sample for the song, "Just for Now" by Imogen Heap was originally used in Lil B and Clams Casino's song "I'm God". Clams revealed in a interview at the 2011 Red Bull Music Academy that he created many beats with this sample including one he would send to Lil B, but he thought it was too fast, but liked half of the song that was not fast so Clams Casino took that part off and it became Lil B's song "I'm the Devil". After Rocky and Clams Casino met he gave the fast part of the song and gave it to Rocky for "Bass" two years later.

Name:
💎💿 LIVE.LOVE.A$AP [Mixtape]
(exec. prod. A$AP Rocky & A$AP Yams)
(LiveLoveA$AP, Live. Love. A$AP)
Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server: Rocky's first ever project titled "LIVE.LOVE.A$AP" released on Halloween, or October 31st, 2011. This mixtape is widely known for its slowed down, Houstan influenced, psychedelic and dream-like sounds which made it stand out from many other hip-hop projects from this time period and it would become a gigantic pioneer for the genre of cloud rap for many years to come. Much later in 2021 the mixtape was re-released on streaming services, missing certain songs such as "Kissin' Pink" "Out Of This World" and "Purple Swag: Chapter 2" along with featuring different versions of the songs "Leaf" and "Acid Drip", but added a new song titled “Sandman” dedicated to the late A$AP Yams with production by long time collaborator Clams Casino along with a newer producer Kelvin Krash.

The song was then released not long after on Rocky's mixtape "LIVE.LOVE.A$AP" which was released on October 31st, 2011 along with being track one on the re-release version which was released on October 29th, 2021. The song was written by A$AP Rocky, Clams Casino, Mike Ratledge and Karl Jenkins, mixed by Pat Viala and mastered by Dan Millice and Tatsuya Sato. The song also samples "Adiemus" by Karl Jenkins. According to the creator of the fake "Deep Purple" compilation, who had influence on "Mouth Fulla Gold" and "LIVE.LOVE.A$AP" through connecting with A$AP Yams, "Palace" and "Wassup" were sent to him prior to release to show people and see how they felt about the track meaning this song was seemingly made prior to the day "Wassup" was released on May 21st, 2011.

💎 Wassup
(prod. Clams Casino)
Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server: A producer based in New Jersey named Clams Casino found Rocky online in April 2011, likely through "Purple Swag" and reached out. This is when he found out that he was Rocky's favorite producer and already recorded over the March 7th, 2011 instrumental "Numb" which would become "Demons" on Rocky's "LIVE.LOVE.A$AP" mixtape. After finally meeting each other they recorded their first collaboration together titled "Wassup" which was released online on May 21st, 2011. The song was written by A$AP Rocky, Clams Casino and Mimi Goese, mixed by Pat Viala and mastered by Dan Millice and Tatsuya Sato. This song is the first instance of the "I be that pretty motherfucker, Harlem's what I'm reppin'" line that would later be reused on Rocky's break out hit titled "Peso". The song was possibly intended for the "Mouth Fulla Gold" as it was being worked on during the time of release, but this is not confirmed. The track was featured as track four on Rocky's October 31st, 2011 mixtape "LIVE.LOVE.A$AP", and also appeared as track four on the tenth anniversary version of the album that released on October 29th, 2021.

Name:
⭐️ Brand New Guy
(feat. ScHoolboy Q) (prod. Don Cannon & Lyle Leduff)
Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server: In a September 2nd, 2023 interview BACKONFIGG ScHoolboy Q confirmed that Rocky reached out to him as he was one of his favorite rappers, but ScHoolboy Q did not know exactly who Rocky was yet and did not agree to work with him. That was until Top Dawg Entertainment founder anthony "Top Dawg" Tiffith and filmmaker Dave Free told him about Rocky, likely through seeing the "Purple Swag" music video which was released on July 5th, 2011. That is when ScHoolboy Q then listened to his music and enjoyed it. Rocky contacted him again as he stated in a January 17th, 2012 interview that he started to meet people who knew people who could get him in contact with him. Because ScHoolboy Q was in New York City at the time he accepted to work with him and told him to come to the studio he was at, which is when Rocky and A$AP Yams went. After meeting Rocky played ScHoolboy Q a track titled "Brand New Guy" which Rocky already had the idea for, but ScHoolboy Q did not like it at first. This is because in his own words he was "just hating because [Rocky] came in fly" as he was wearing Adidas Jeremy Scott shoes at the time. The ego ScHoolboy Q had went away due to Rocky's energy and then he quickly wrote his verse and just "cracked". After this, Rocky wanted to go back and forth on a verse which they did and the song was complete. In a Facebook post by Rocky from July 18th, 2011 states "ASAP Rocky x Schoolboy Q coming soon" with the two in the studio while Rocky has Jeremy Scott Adidas shoes on, seemingly confiming the song was recorded on this day. The song was then finally released as track five on Rocky's mixtape "LIVE.LOVE.A$AP" which was released on October 31st, 2011. The song was written by A$AP Rocky, ScHoolboy Q, Don Cannon and Lyle Leduff, mixed by Pat Viala, mastered by Dan Millice and Tatsuya Sato and engineered by Don Cannon. The song samples "Tyler’s Rage" by Frederic Talgorn and a line from Lil Wayne's song titled "John". The track had a music video directed by The ICU and Rocky that was recorded in New York City and was released on June 4th, 2012. The song appeared again as track five on the ten year anniversary re-release of "LIVE.LOVE.A$AP" which was released on October 29th, 2021.

Name:
⭐️ Purple Swag: Chapter 2
(feat. A$AP Nast & SpaceGhostPurrp) (prod. A$AP Ty Beats) (add. prod. A$AP Rocky)
(Purple Swag (Remix))
Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server: While the song "Purple Swag" did not attract as much popularity as it did later on it did catch the attention of Miami artist SpaceGhostPurrp. He then moved up from Florida to New York City to live with Rocky and A$AP Ty Beats and began to collaborate with the A$AP Mob. While the time frame of when he moved is not completely clear, it was likely sometime in May or June of 2011 as Rocky appeared on SpaceGhostPurrp's June 15th, 2011 song "My Enemie" with background vocals. Now that SpaceGhostPurrp was working with the A$AP Mob more and more a remix to "Purple Swag" with A$AP Nast featured was previewed while closing out the CMJ Showcase at Hiro Ballroom in New York City on A$AP Ferg's birthday, October 20th, 2011. The song which is titled "Purple Swag: Chapter 2" was officially released as track six on Rocky's mixtape "LIVE.LOVE.A$AP" which was released on October 31st, 2011. Something important to note is that the song features different Rocky vocals than the original track. The song was written by A$AP Rocky, A$AP Nast, A$AP Ty Beats, SpaceGhostPurrp and Curtis Williams, mixed by Pat Viala and mastered by Dan Millice and Tatsuya Sato. Along with this the track samples an a nnouncer Voice Clip from the popular video game Mortal Kombat II, "Stay With Me" by Clint Mansell and "Still Tippin'" by Mike Jones. The song was not put onto streaming services for the tenth anniversary re-release of "LIVE.LOVE.A$AP" because of their ongoing fued that started in 2012. The remix was replaced by the original version of the song instead.

Name:
💎 Purple Swag [Single]
(prod. A$AP Ty Beats) (add. prod. A$AP Rocky)
Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server: Similar to various tracks made by Rocky during the 2010 and 2011 time period the song "Purple Swag" was uploaded to the A$AP Yams owned HarlemzMostHated YouTube channel on April 14th, 2011.The song samples 'Still Tippin'" by Mike Jones and a announcer voice clip from the popular videogame Mortal Kombat II. The song was written by A$AP Rocky and A$AP Ty Beats, mixed by Pat Viala and mastered by Dan Millice and Tatsuya Sato. As stated in the April 21st, 2015 episode of Complex’s “Magnum Opus” series Rocky and the producer, who would later go on to by A$AP Ty Beats, met through Facebook and Rocky would add the Mike Jones sample himself. In a November 13th, 2012 interview with Complex Rocky revealed that the song was recorded "in some small ass closet" and revealed he was on substances which acommodate to the slow feeling of the song. According to A$AP Bari in a January 14th, 2023 interview a mixtape before "LIVE.LOVE.A$AP" existed that an engineer that Rocky was working with deleted the whole project and only three songs were recovered. These tracks being "Roll One Up", "Demons" and "Purple Swag". This means this project was made inbetween March 7th, 2011, the day the "Demons" instrumental was released by Clams Casino, and April 14th, 2011. It is evident that the track would appear on the mixtape worked on after this unknown deleted project titled "Mouth Fulla Gold", but this is not tottally confirmed as of right now. The track did not gain much recognition right away until a fan from France named PureBakingSoda would added it as track three to their unofficial compilation mixtape "Deep Purple". Although this mixtape is fake, it would jump-start Rocky's career as it obtained more and more downloads. A music video for the song that was directed by Jason Ano and co-directed by Rocky was dedicated to Rocky's late brother Ricky Mayers was released on July 5th, 2011 and gave Rocky more of a name for himself. In the music video a caucasian woman named Anna Trill mouthed the 'n-word' which spiraled into a small controversy, but this is not the only backlash the song would recive. Many people did not agree with the slowed down Houston inspired sound as Rocky was a New York native but Rocky ignored this and embraced that as that type of music inspired in greatly. The musoc video included a slowed down snippet of Rocky's then upcoming song "Peso" which would become his true breakout hit song. Around July of 2011 would scrap "Mouth Fulla Gold" and begin to work on his "LIVE.LOVE.A$AP" mixtape. When that mixtape released on October 31st, 2011 it featured a remix of the song rather than the original song. This remix was titled "Purple Swag: Chapter 2" and featured SpaceGhostPurrp along with fellow A$AP Mob member A$AP Nast. After Rocky's new found success the song was offiicially relased as a single on December 8th, 2011. On the tenth anniversary edition of "LIVE.LOVE.A$AP" whixh was released on October 29th, 2021 the remix "Purple Swag: Chapter 2" did not appear and the original song "Purple Swag" was featured as track six on that version of the mixtape, likely due to Rocky and SpaceGhostPurrp's fued that started in 2012.

Type: After the massive success of "LIVE.LOVE.A$AP" and a revolutionary three million dollar record deal, Rocky decided to go full force into his first studio album seemingly right away. While being a part of the Club Paradise Tour with Drake and Kendrick Lamar he began to make connections with bigger names in the industry. In an interview with HipHopDX on November 29th, 2011 Rocky talked about making a compilation album with the A$AP Mob then titled "LONG.LIVE.A$AP", indicating that the solo album around that time was named something entirely different. By March 2012, the album was still being worked on and was rumored to be titled "A$AP Rocky Forever Part 1", which was quickly debunked and was now titled "LONG.LIVE.A$AP", with the Mob's project being renamed to "Trilluminati".

Name:
Lloyd Banks - Make It Stack
(feat. A$AP Rocky) (prod. Doe Pesci & Tha Jerm)
Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server: “Make It Stack” is a song by Queens, New York City artist Lloyd Banks, released as track six on his “Cold Corner 2” mixtape on November 8th, 2011, featuring A$AP Rocky (his first release since "LIVE.LOVE.A$AP"). The track features classic early 2010s G-Unit style production by Doe Pesci and The Jerm, with the song’s main themes being about hustling, stacking money, and street dominance. Rocky’s guest verse has complex lyrical content, which he discussed in a November 13th, 2012 article with Complex titled 'A$AP Rocky Breaks Down His 15 Most Essential Songs,' where he said, “The Lloyd Banks thing was me on some lyrical shit at this time, which was when nobody believed it. It was like, ‘Okay, now let me prove to y’all that I can spit.’ So that’s why I did what I did with Lloyd Banks.” After the initial November 8th, 2011 drop of the "Cold Corner 2" mixtape it was re-released onto SoundCloud by Lloyd Banks on February 22nd, 2012 and was later put onto streaming services.

Name:
⭐️ Juicy J - Real Hustlers Don’t Sleep
(feat. A$AP Rocky & SpaceGhostPurrp) (prod. Big Jerm & I.D. Labs)
Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server: A song dedicated to nonstop grinding, "Real Hustlers Don't Sleep" was released as track twenty-four on Three Six Mafia co-founder Juicy J's mixtape "Blue Dream & Lean" which was released on November 30th, 2011 with features from then upcoming underground artists A$AP Rocky and SpaceGhostPurrp with loud and hard-hitting trap production by Big Jerm and I.D. Labs. The tracks features are especially notable being one of the only officially released collaborations between A$AP Rocky and SpaceGhostPurrp before they had beef. Their verses are significant due to the Memphis influence in A$AP Rocky's verse which mentions the twos duo name and common nickname for each other, 'T.W.I.N.' (two wild ignorant niggas), and the comedic elements heard in SpaceGhostPurrp's verse which ranges from countless sex-related references, taking over the country and lines like "Hi guys, what's up, bitch, my name is Purrp, I'm fucking your bitch, and I made her squirt". A few years later, this song was referenced in the lyrics of A$AP Rocky's October 3rd, 2014 single titled "Multiply" (which features Juicy J) in the [blank] verse of the song saying "real hustlers don't sleep, take cat naps", directly referring to the songs title.

The song was uploaded in very low quality compared to the original release and the very end of the track cuts off before it can finish. It is unknown why Seth Narley uploaded this low quality version, but it is still on streaming services to this day. On the same day this song released as a single, it was featured as track ten on Seth Narley's "The Sigma Fly EP", which features the same version with poor quality.

Type: In March 2012, A$AP Rocky, OG Ron C and The Chopstars dropped Live Love Purple, a Chopped Not Slopped remix of A$AP Rocky’s breakout mixtape LIVE.LOVE.A$AP. It took Rocky’s already trippy, atmospheric sound and ran it through Houston’s signature chopped and screwed filter, slowing it down, stretching it out, and giving it that syrupy Southern vibe. Rocky’s love for Southern rap was already clear, but Live Love Purple made that connection even louder. One standout moment is the "Purple Swag" remix, which features Texas rap icons Bun B, Paul Wall, and Killa Kyleon, adding more hometown flavor.

Name:
Lord$ Never Worry
(Collaboration with A$AP Mob)
(TRILLUMINATI, LONG.LIVE.A$AP)
Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server:
(Late October 2011) (The A$AP Mob's LIVELOVEA$AP Tumblr page changed to LONGLIVEA$AP)
(November 29th, 2011) (An A$AP Mob compilation mixtape titled "LONG.LIVE.A$AP" is mentioned in a HipHopDX interview, which was planned to be released after the deluxe to "LIVE.LOVE.A$AP")
(February 2012) (Mixtape is set to release but is delayed to April, Yams claims the compilation is untitled)
(March 2012) (Mixtape is delayed from April to May)
(March 26th, 2012) (Told Ya is released and TRILLUMINATI title is revealed, this same week the Mob moves to Los Angeles to work on the project)
(May 2nd, 2012) (A$AP Yams explains there is no motivation to work on the project in Los Angeles so they plan to move back to New York to get inspired)
(May 2012) (Title gets changed to Lord$ Never Worry)
(August 28th, 2012) (Lord$ Never Worry is released)
Type: With Rocky on the rise with massive success from his mixtape “LIVE.LOVE.A$AP” it only made sense for the A$AP Mob to all make a project together so more of the other members of A$AP could be recognized for their talents, and they did just that. The project was first announced by Rocky in a November 29th, 2011 interview with HipHopDX with him stating “Yeah, “LONG.LIVE.A$AP.” That’s like the joint with everybody on it. It’s a compilation. I plan on dropping that after “LIVE.LOVE.A$AP” drops again, because we gonna do a deluxe version of “LIVE.LOVE.A$AP”. Right after that, we gonna drop the A$AP compilation with everybody on it.” The project has been described as a compilation at the time and the title would later become the name of Rocky’s first studio album. The project was said for a February 2012 release which was eventually pushed back for an April release but by the time March came around a May release was revealed. This was revealed in a Tumblr post from the A$AP Mob speaking on the release of A$AP Ant and Bodega Bamz song “Told Ya” on March 26th, 2012 states “The baby goon of the year A$AP Ant brings you his first visual alongside Bodega Bamz produced by DJ Carnage. Look out for TRILLUMINVTI this May featuring new music from Pretty Flacko, Nasty Baby 90’s Nigga, Twelvy the Good Goon, Fergenstein aka Diamond Dolla$ Page, and the rest of the Mob”. In this post a new name for the project can be seen “Trilluminati” and again, was set to be released in May 2012. A fan asked where the project was in April and Yams responded on his Tumblr on May 2nd, 2012  saying that the A$AP Mob thought moving to LA “would be the move”, but it ended up being the total opposite. He claimed “The energy is dead here there was no motivation here to make music”. He then said they’re going back to New York City to get the inspiration they needed again, Yams stated “the music will be rawer than ever believe that....TRILLUMINATI will be here” again confirming the title. With the project still not coming out in May many still wondered if it would be released any time soon, finally in July Yams announced the first single for the album “Bath Salt” which was released on July 27th. Yams stated there were no more delays and the album would come out on August 28th officially announcing the title “Lord$ Never Worry”. Before this another single “Purple Kisses” released on August 13th and the last single “Work” released on August 20th. Finally, after all the delays “Lord$ Never Worry” released on the planned date of August 28th, 2012.

Type: After the massive success of Rocky’s first mixtape and a revolutionary three million dollar record deal he decided to go full force into his first studio album seemingly right away. While still being a part of the Club Paradise Tour with Drake and Kendrick Lamar he began to make connections with bigger names in the industry. Not too long after the release of the hit mixtape Rocky talked about compilation album with the A$AP Mob then titled "LONG.LIVE.A$AP". Still working on his own album by March 2012 Rocky's album name was rumored to be "A$AP Rocky Forever Part 1" which was quickly debunked and was now titled "LONG.LIVE.A$AP" with the A$AP Mob's project being renamed to "Trilluminati". At this point the album was planned to drop on July 4th, 2012, but the album would later be delayed. Fans were hungry for more solo Rocky music as he had been featuring on a handful of songs up until April 16th, 2012 when the first single for his album "Goldie" released with production from a very well respected producer Hit-Boy. The album was delayed once the July release day hit for unknown reasons, but Rocky still worked on the album with a second single titled "Fuckin' Problems" releasing on October 24th, 2012. This song has the likes of Drake, Kendrick Lamar and 2 Chainz and quickly became Rocky's most popular song at that point, and remains as one of them to this day. It was then announced in December 2012 that the album would release on January 15th, 2013 with the title track "Long Live A$AP" releasing as a single on December 18th, 2012. Just two days later on December 20th, 2012 a listening event for the album took place at a local Harlem music store. The album was completely finished and ready to release on its planned day, this was when just two days after that on December 22nd, 2012 the whole album was leaked online for everyone to hear. Surprisingly, Rocky was not upset by this, infact he was completely okay with it as long as people liked the album as he spent so much time making it. Just four days before the album the last pre-release single titled "Wild for the Night" with Skrillex and Birdy Nam Nam, which was also a huge hit. The album then released on the planned date of January 15th, 2013 along with a deluxe edition adding four more songs to the tracklist. The album is credited for its creative direction as well as big time features after being for the most part unknown not too long before the release. Despite this, Rocky knew the songs such as "Fuckin' Problems" and "Wild for the Night" were made as hits for his record label in a sense and knew he wanted to create a more personal and creative album next.

Name:
L.O.R.D.
(Collaboration with A$AP Mob)
(L.O.R.D.S.)
(exec. prod. A$AP Yams)
Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server:
(August 28th, 2012) (Lord$ Never Worry is released)
(August 26th, 2013) (Album is announced by A$AP Ferg during the VMAs)
(September 26th, 2014) (A$AP Yams confirms L.O.R.D. is scrapped)
Type: During a A$AP Ferg interview during the VMAs on August 26th, 2013 a upcoming, then untitled, project from the A$AP Mob was revealed by one of the the founders A$AP Yams to be releasing on October 15th, 2013, but on that date Yams revealed that he only revealed the initial release date because Amazon had leaked the target date without it being confirmed. He went on to say the album was pushed back because of features and sample clearances. Then the first single for the album was released on December 4th, 2013 which was “Trillmatic” with A$AP Nast and Method Man with the album's second single coming out not long after this time being titled “See Me” and only featuring A$AP Ant. The album was said to be finished on February 20th, 2014 with a new March 4th, 2014 release date. That release date slowly approached and it eventually became delayed until sometime in April with the album's third single releasing on April 22nd, 2014 titled “Xscape” featuring only A$AP Twelvyy. The album continued to be worked on and the fourth single was released on June 6th, 2014 featuring the likes of A$AP Rocky, A$AP Ferg, A$AP Twelvyy and A$AP Nast titled “Hella Hoes”. After the release of that last single the album’s development went relatively radio silent for the most part until September 26th, 2014 when A$AP Yams confirmed on his Tumblr account that “L.O.R.D.” was officially scrapped. The reasons he gave as for what reason the project was scrapped was there was “too many energies” as it was “hard to get everybody on the same page. He then stated that the best tracks from the album would eventually make their way onto Rocky, Twelvvy and Nast’s solo albums, it’s possible that some made their way onto “AT.LONG.LAST.A$AP” in 2015 after Yams’ unfortunate passing, but that is unclear. The solo project from Twelvvy “12” which was released in 2017 only features one confirmed song from “L.O.R.D.”, and Nast never has released a solo project throughout his entire career. As previously stated, A$AP Yams passed away on January 18th, 2015 and even though “L.O.R.D.” was scrapped the A$AP Mob got together to make the first album in the “Cozy Tapes” series “Cozy Tapes: Vol. 1 Friends -“ on October 31st, 2016.

Type: When Rocky’s second album “AT.LONG.LAST.A$AP” was in production it was first going to have a similar idea to its predecessor until the tragic death of Rocky’s friend and mentor A$AP Yams in January 18th, 2015. After this devastating event he changed the entire concept of the album to a new direction; he had some lesser known features such as a homeless man from London named Joe Fox who featured on a plethora of tracks while still having big names such as Kanye West and Lil Wayne. The project again gained attention through its singles such as “L$D” and “Everyday” among many other songs on the album.

Name:
Wavy Wednesdays
(Collaboration with A$AP Mob & AWGE)
Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server:
(January 16th, 2016) (The Wavy Wednesdays series begins)
(March 30th, 2016) (The last Wavy Wednesdays loosie is released)
(April 27th, 2016) (COZY BOYS WAVY WEDNESDAY MIX is released on SoundCloud)
Type: As promotion to build up hype the then upcoming debut A$AP MOB album, Cozy Tapes Vol. 1: Friends -, AWGE along with the rest of A$AP MOB put together Wavy Wednesdays as loosies that would come out each Wednesday on AWGE's SoundCloud to build up hype and give out A$AP material, old and new. The promotion was announced alongside the release of the long-awaited A$AP Rocky remix to Drake's Wu-Tang Forever. The run lasted 6 weeks, with none of the songs released for Wavy Wednesdays ending up on Cozy Tapes Vol. 1. The cover used is a mix/playlist on AWGE's SoundCloud, released and curated by A$AP MOB.

Name:
💎💿 Cozy Tapes Vol. 1: Friends - [Album]
(with A$AP Mob & AWGE)
Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server: Around the time of A$AP Yams' unfortunate death in January of 2015 the A$AP Mob decided to produce an album as a whole group, with this being the first one since 2012’s ‘Lord$ Never Worry’. The title of this project was titled ‘Cozy Tapes Vol. 1: Friends -‘ with the friends in the title signifying the many other collaborators outside of the mob including people such as Tyler, The Creator, Playboi Carti and even Lil Uzi Vert. This project gained lots of success through songs like ‘Telephone Calls’ and ‘Yamborghini High’, two of the singles for the tape, the success of this project motivated the A$AP Mob to release a second volume in the future.

Name:
💎 💿 TESTING [Album]
(exec. prod. A$AP Rocky, AWGE, Chase Infinite & Héctor Delgado)
Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server: Similar to Rocky’s previous solo project he totally changed the direction of his third studio album “TESTING”, for example the well known psychedelic production of Rocky’s music now had a more industrial feel and the style in overall took a big turn compared to his last release. He worked with many new faces such as Dean Blunt and Kelvin Krash for this project making a big difference. The album saw some success through songs such as ‘Praise The Lord (Da Shine)’ which is now Rocky’s most popular song to date, though was not praised as much as his past releases and failed to chart number one on the Billboard 200, which motivated him to change his sound on his next release.

Name:
WANG$AP
(Collaboration with Tyler, The Creator)

💎 Potato Salad
(with Tyler, The Creator) (prod. Kanye West & Missy Elliot)
(Knock Knock, Sexydick)
Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server: “Potato Salad” is a track released on AWGE’s AWGE DVD Vol. 3, which was released on July 23, 2018. The song was put on streaming services on September 28, 2018. On the track, we hear A$AP Rocky and Tyler, The Creator exchange bars on their successes, clothing and money. The track’s instrumental is drawn from “Knock Knock” by Monica, which was produced by Kanye West. In a tweet Tyler stated that he was “trying to rap on that for years.” The track also teases a potential collaboration between the two, to be called WANG$AP. Though it is not confirmed and Tyler had said not to get our hopes up, the collaboration is still a possiblity. They previously teased their merging on “WHAT THE FUCK RIGHT NOW” and “Who Dat Boy”, respectively. “WHAT THE FUCK RIGHT NOW”: They ain’t ready for the WANG$AP, niggas! “Who Dat Boy”: WANG$AP on the bumper sticker, fuck you niggas.

Name: ⭐️💿 LIVE.LOVE.A$AP (Re-Release)
Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server: In October 2021, A$AP Rocky commemorated the 10th anniversary of his debut mixtape, LIVE.LOVE.A$AP by releasing it on major streaming platforms for the first time. The 2021 re-release introduced a new track titled "Sandman," produced by Kelvin Krash and long time collaborator Clams Casino. However, due to sample clearance issues, it omitted fan favorites like "Kissin' Pink" and "Out of This World", while the song "Purple Swag: Chapter 2" was replaced with the original "Purple Swag". Additionally, the mixtape's artwork was updated from its original black-and-white design to a colorized version, featuring a purple birthmark on Rocky's cheek as a tribute to the late A$AP Yams and as a reference to Rocky's 2015 sophomore album AT.LONG.LAST.A$AP.

This version features a new outro and has additional sound effects playing throughout the music video. It was revealed by Fritz Owens that Carti did not give clearance for his feature which was causing tension between Rocky and Carti affiliates, as he seemingly released the song without letting anyone know. Much later in 2023, when Rocky was asked about the song in an interview, he explained that he posted it because "They just kept leaking it, so we just said 'fuck it' and put it out", despite the fact that the song was completely unheard up until the moment Rocky posted it himself.

Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server: Song titled "I Smoked Away My Brain (I’m God x Demons Mashup)" released as a single on August 18th, 2023. The song is a mash-up of A$AP Rocky's 2011 song "Demons" and Clams Casino's song "I'm God" which was originally known for being the instrumental to the Lil B song of the same name. The original remix, which got popular on social media platforms such as TikTok and YouTube, was made by community member Zanthin, but he unfortunately did not get credit on the official release. Written by A$AP Rocky, Billy Steinberg, Claire D&#39, Uabaldo, Imogen Heap and Rick Nowels. Mixed by Johnny Juliano. Mastered by Johnny Juliano. Samples "Just for Now" by Imogen Heap.

(feat. John Legend) (add. A$AP Rocky & Morgan Freeman) (prod. DJ Moon, johan lenox, Metro Boomin, Peter Lee Johnson & TM88)
Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server: “On Time” serves as the first track on Atlanta-based record producer Metro Boomin’s sophomore studio album HEROES & VILLAINS. The intro is accompanied by vocals from American singer, and record producer John Legend, American actor, director, and narrator Morgan Freeman and New York rapper, music producer and record executive A$AP Rocky.

Name:
Metro Boomin - I Can’t Save You (Interlude)
(feat. 21 Savage, Future & Don Toliver) (add. A$AP Rocky & Young Thug) (prod. Allen Ritter, D. Rich & Metro Boomin)
Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server: In, “I Can’t Save You (Interlude),” Metro Boomin and Future recruit Don Toliver for a mellow, trap interlude in which Don raps about the effect of drugs on their bodies. In contrast, Future expands on the album’s title, HEROES & VILLAINS — showing his villainous personality as he refuses to “save” anyone. In the track’s intro, a single word is said by Young Thug to smoothen the transition between this track and the previous one, “Metro Spider.” The outro samples “No Advance,” a previous collaboration between Metro and 21 Savage; it also includes additional vocals from A$AP Rocky.

Name:

(with A$AP Rocky) (feat. Takeoff) (prod. Metro Boomin & Thundercat)
Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server: In “Feel The Fiyaaaah,” Metro Boomin recruits A$AP Rocky and the late Takeoff for a slow-tempo trap song in which A$AP and Takeoff make correlations between several viral events and their accompanying letter in the alphabet. In the song, both artists make specific references to Adam and Eve and the forbidden fruit and Jesus, while also making references to Gunna and Future’s “pushin P.”

⭐️ Future & Metro Boomin - Show of Hands
(feat. A$AP Rocky) (prod. Honorable C.N.O.T.E. & Metro Boomin)
Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server: On Show of Hands, Future is joined by A$AP Rocky to deliver a fast, boastful track. Although this is the first collaboration between Future and A$AP Rocky since 2016, where they teamed up on A$AP Ferg’s remix of New Level, A$AP Rocky featured on Metro Boomin’s soundtrack for Spiderman: Across the Spider-Verse, on tracks Am I Dreaming and Nonviolent Communication. A$AP Rocky was also a part of HEROES & VILLAINS, providing voice lines and interludes throughout the album, and delivering an amazing verse on the final track, Feel the Fiyaaaah. Rocky’s presence on the track could also be a nod to his longtime beef with Drake, who Future has fired multiple shots towards on both WE DON’T TRUST YOU and WE STILL DON’T TRUST YOU.

(with A$AP Rocky & PlayThatBoiZay) (prod. Kwas Darko)
Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server: “HOODLUMZ” is the third and final single leading up to Denzel Curry’s second instalment in his “King of The Mischievous South” series. Released on July 16th 2024, “HOODLUMZ” features rappers PlayThatBoiZay, who delivers a rough and raw hook over the hardstyle synths, and A$AP Rocky, who comes through in the latter half of the song and delivers a fast, catchy verse where he raps about guns, drugs, his friends in the hood. Denzel curry first announced the track on his Instagram and Twitter (X) social media accounts on July 15th, revealing it to be the final single leading up to the anticipation to his first album since 2022’s “Melt My Eyez, See Your Future.” “King of the Mischievous South Vol. 2” a sequel to Denzel’s second mixtape and first ever Raider Klan release, “King of the Mischievous South Vol. 1 Underground Tape 1996”, the track was made also to corroborate the Raider Klan & A$AP Mob beef ending (excluding SGP). The track released on July 19th 2024.

Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server: After the release of "Tailor Swif" Rocky would later suprise release "Ruby Rosary" featuring J. Cole with productiion by The Alchemist. Song features light jabs at Drake. The Japanese text on this song cover art roughly translates to, At the top “エイサップ・ロッキー・プレゼンツ” ASAP Rocky Presents, Above the Y “世界中で” Around the World, In red font “バカにしないでーこの秋もうすぐ登場” Don’t be Dumb – coming soon this fall!, Text at bottom “1980年代のニューヨークの暗い路地で、A$APロッキーという名の殺人者が恐怖を撒き散らしていた。黒い服に身を包み、夜の街をさまよい、高 層ビルの影で獲物を追い詰める。その名は都市伝説のように街中でささやかれ、最も強靭な者でさえも恐怖に震えた。次にどこで襲うのか、誰も 予測できず、街は恐怖に包まれていた” "In the dark alleys of 1980s New York City, a killer named A$AP Rocky spreads terror. Dressed in black, he roamed the streets at night, hunting down his prey in the shadows of skyscrapers. His name was whispered throughout the city like an urban legend, and even the mightiest of men trembled with fear. No one could predict where it would strike next, and the city was enveloped in fear."

Name:
⭐️ Tailor Swif
(prod. Hitkidd)
(Taylor Swift, Wetty)
Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server: After the delay of "DON'T BE DUMB" Rocky would release the previously leaked song "Tailor Swif". The title of the track is an obvious play on the name Taylor Swfit with the T being removed from Swift and Taylor being changed to Tailor. The song was first performed/previewed at Rolling Loud Portugal on July 7th, 2022. The song leaked in its entirety alongside the music video on July 9th, 2022. Then performed again at Postivus Festival on July 16th, 2022. Rocky confirmed in 2023 that the song won’t appear on the album due to it leaking but played it at Rolling Loud Miami 2023 not long after. The song’s cover art appeared on merch and on Rocky’s Instagram in early 2023, but nothing ever came from this until the reworked song and cover were leaked in September of 2023. Revealed by Mod to have always been titled “Tailor Swif”. On August 30th, 2024, the song and its video saw an offical release on streaming platforms.

Name:

(prod. Kelvin Krash)

HIGHJACK (right back)
(with Alok & Łaszewo) (prod. Alok & Matthew Ehrlich)
Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server: After a five-month hiatus from releasing music, A$AP Rocky returned on May 16, 2025, with a completely unannounced EDM remix of his August 2, 2024 single “HIGHJACK,” made in collaboration with DJ Alok and indie group Łaszewo. This remix does not feature any vocals from the collaborators on the original song, including Jessica Pratt, Creed B Good, and Jon Batiste. The track primarily repeats the line “A$AP right up on my gut, and that's a life tat,” while also including the lyric “Walk inside the booth, I just talk, I don’t write raps,” over a fast, club-focused beat that transforms the original song's tone into a brighter, more energetic style. The song has performed well on streaming, amassing over 17 million streams on Spotify, though fan reception has been somewhat lukewarm.

Name:
AIR FORCE (BLACK DEMARCO)
(prod. A$AP Rocky, Zach Fogarty & Manny Laurenko)
Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server: Track 11 on "Don''t Be Dumb". Digital Nas is officially credited as a producer, but according to himself he didn't work on the song, and it was produced by Manny Larenko. Manny himself confirmed he worked on the song and claimed in messages with a fan that Digital Nas was given credit instead of him because he passed the beat to Rocky. Co-produced by Zach Fogarty. Additional production by Jordan Patrick. Includes background vocals from Spencer Sutherland and Car!ton. Vocals recorded by Gosha Usov. Mixed by MIKE DEAN. Mastered by Tatsuya Sato and Ray Bergin.
